key_insight: ToMap makes proof autoformalization more efficient by spending test-time optimization mainly on the Decomposer, since high-quality atomic proof units are the key bottleneck for downstream formalization and proving.
engineering_relevance: The paper offers a practical test-time compute allocation strategy for theorem proving systems, improving accuracy and semantic fidelity while reducing inference cost.
method: Decomposer-Formalizer-Prover pipeline for long-horizon proof autoformalization
Bottleneck analysis identifies decomposition quality as the main failure point
GEPA-inspired prompt evolution uses formal verification progress plus semantic proof rubrics to guide decomposition refinement
evidence_signal: On ProofFlowBench, ToMap reports a 19.0% improvement over the prior best method under both syntactic correctness and semantic faithfulness, with lower test-time cost.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliable formal-verification feedback and rubric design, so gains may be sensitive to prover quality and the number of refinement iterations chosen.
paper_title: Efficient Test-Time Optimization for Multi-Agent Proof Autoformalization
